Thought 3: Alfred had the means (flamethrower), opportunity (access to Adam's room on full moon nights), and was present at the scene (motel) on the night of the murder. However, he had no stated motive. Gregory had a strong motive (blackmail), means (owned a flamethrower and was trained to use it), and was also present at the motel bar on the night of the murder. But the key point is the murder weapon: Alfred's flamethrower was found in his tool bag after the murder, and he was seen carrying it to and from the motel on full moon nights. If Alfred committed the murder, why would he still have the flamethrower in his bag afterward? It seems unlikely he would keep the murder weapon on him if he were guilty. Gregory, on the other hand, had his flamethrower at home, and there's no mention of it being missing or used. Also, the police found the flamethrower in Alfred's bag based on a tip, which might be suspicious—perhaps someone framed Alfred? But the narrative doesn't suggest that. Alternatively, Gregory could have used his own flamethrower and left it at home, but then why would Alfred's flamethrower be involved? Wait, let's re-read the murder weapon description.
